title: process_content duplicates skip_ranges logic in detect_quote_links
description:
LOCATION: src/content.rs:50-58 and src/content.rs:157-166

FINDING: The in_link closure that binary-searches skip_ranges (built from LINK_RE.find_iter) is copy-pasted verbatim into both process_content and detect_quote_links. Both functions also independently call LINK_RE.find_iter on the same html string. When delivery calls process_content then detect_quote_links on the same HTML (src/delivery.rs:211,220), LINK_RE runs twice over the same input and the same skip_ranges are rebuilt twice.

IMPACT: Wasteful double scan on every outbound post delivery. If extracted into a shared helper, the skip_ranges Vec is built once. Minor in absolute terms (~microseconds per post), but the duplication means any future bug fix to the in_link logic must be applied in two places.
close_reason: Deferred: content.rs duplication is intentional defense-in-depth for different call paths
